errors: [agent_error] AutoRound's Gemma4 MoE block-wise quantization has a bug in its position embedding caching: cached position_embeddings from layer 0 (seq_len=512) have 256-dim RoPE tensors, but subsequent layers' attention expects 512-dim RoPE tensors. The bug is in _prepare_gemma4_replay_inputs which incorrectly caches/reuses per-layer rotary embeddings across layers.
RuntimeError: The size of tensor a (512) must match the size of tensor b (256) at non-singleton dimension 3

Root cause: Auto-round's Gemma4 block quantization uses a shared caching mechanism for position embeddings that is incompatible with Gemma4's RoPE implementation. When the cached position_embeddings from block 0 are computed at one sequence length (512) but a subsequent layer's attention expects a different sequence length (256), the RoPE cos/sin tensors have mismatched dimensions.

The bug manifests in _prepare_gemma4_replay_inputs when it retrieves cached position_embeddings and passes them to rotary_emb, which then calls the Gemma4 apply_rotary_pos_emb with mismatched tensor shapes.
solutions: Fix requires a patch to auto_round/compressors/base.py or special_model_handler.py to ensure position_embeddings are recomputed per-layer rather than cached and reused when sequence lengths differ.
Alternative: Use device_map='auto' with batch_size=1 as a workaround, though this was not tested due to time constraints.
Alternative: Use a different export format (GGUF/GPTQ/AWQ) that does not use block-wise quantization with input caching.
Alternative: Wait for an auto-round fix that addresses the Gemma4 MoE + block quantization + RoPE interaction.
root_cause: AutoRound's Gemma4 MoE block-wise quantization has a bug in its position embedding caching: cached position_embeddings from layer 0 (seq_len=512) have 256-dim RoPE tensors, but subsequent layers' attention expects 512-dim RoPE tensors. The bug is in _prepare_gemma4_replay_inputs which incorrectly caches/reuses per-layer rotary embeddings across layers.
suggested_fix: AutoRound's Gemma4 MoE + block-wise quantization with NVFP4 scheme is fundamentally incompatible with this model (gemma-4-A4B-98e-v6-coder-it). The root cause is in auto_round/compressors/special_model_handler.py _prepare_gemma4_replay_inputs which caches position_embeddings at layer 0 with incorrect RoPE dimensions and reuses them for later layers. Retry will not help. Suggested alternatives: (1) Use llm_compressor format instead of auto_round for NVFP4, (2) Use W4A16 scheme instead of NVFP4 which uses full-model rather than block-wise quantization, (3) Use a different quantization tool such as GPTQModel or AWQ that does not use block-wise calibration with cached position embeddings.
